name: nuqs (Type-safe URL Search Params State Manager)

performance.notes: URL serialization only; no network overhead

agent_readiness.notes: Type-safe URL search params as React state. useQueryState('page', parseAsInteger.withDefault(1)) returns [value, setValue]. Built-in parsers: parseAsString, parseAsInteger, parseAsFloat, parseAsBoolean, parseAsJson, parseAsArrayOf, parseAsTimestamp. Supports Next.js App Router (shallow routing), Pages Router, Remix, React Router. Batching with useQueryStates() for multiple params. Server-side parsing for RSC/SSR. Shareable URL state — replaces useState for filterable tables, pagination, search.

gotchas: URL has size limits (avoid storing large objects)
URL params visible to users and server logs
Shallow routing creates history entries (use shallow: false carefully)
Next.js App Router requires NuqsAdapter in layout
best_when: Tables and data views where users can share filtered results
Next.js App Router apps needing type-safe search params
Replacing useState for URL-persistable UI state
avoid_when: Large state that doesn't fit in URL
summary: nuqs provides type-safe URL search params as React state. useQueryState('page', parseAsInteger) returns [value, setValue] with URL persistence. Built-in parsers for all common types. Works with Next.js App Router, Pages Router, Remix, React Router. Server-side parsing for RSC. Essential for shareable filter tables, pagination, and search states. Replaces useState for any URL-persistable UI state.
